name: Kettlebell Side Bend
resistance_profile: Dynamic lateral flexion — the obliques and QL on the side away from the weight are loaded in a deep stretch at the bottom, then shorten to bend you back upright.
cues: Bend straight sideways, don't rotate or lean forward
Slide the bell down the outside of the thigh
Control the stretch, then crunch the side back up
execution_steps: Stand tall holding a kettlebell in one hand at your side, feet hip-width.
Keep the hips square and facing forward — no twisting or leaning forward or back.
Lower the bell down the side of your leg by bending only sideways at the waist.
Feel a stretch through the top-side obliques, then contract to bend back up to tall and slightly past.
Complete the reps, then switch the bell to the other hand.
good_when: You want to load the obliques through a full lateral-flexion range
You want a lengthened-biased oblique exercise
bad_when: Your lower back dislikes side-bending under load
You can't keep the movement purely in the frontal plane
common_errors: Rotating or hinging forward instead of pure side bending
Using too heavy a bell and cutting the range short
